text: The surgeon places a laparoscopic adjustable gastric band, a silicone ring, around the top portion of the stomach, creating a small pouch above the band.
The surgeon identifies the anatomical area at the top portion of the stomach where the laparoscopic adjustable gastric band will be positioned.

Oftentimes we'll find a hiatal hernia, which we will fix as part of the procedure. This prevents any reflux after surgery and yet still allows for small amounts to be eaten in a comfortable environment.
After repairing the hiatal hernia, the surgeon creates a tunnel behind the stomach for the placement of the laparoscopic adjustable gastric band.
The surgeon creates a small tunnel behind the top portion of the stomach or esophagus using laparoscopic instruments, ensuring the procedure is performed under direct vision for safety.

A special band passing instrument is maneuvered around the stomach, allowing the laparoscopic adjustable gastric band to be placed within the abdominal cavity. The band is then attached to the instrument to be safely positioned around the stomach.
The surgeon positions the band around the stomach, forming a small virtual pouch above the band with a capacity of fifteen to twenty cubic centimeters.
Some people compare that to the size of a plum or a golf ball, but it's a very small pouch.
The surgeon assembles the gastric band inside the abdominal cavity, securing it in place around the stomach.
The surgeon secures the band in position by sewing a portion of the stomach over the band to the pouch above it, ensuring the band remains properly aligned despite its balloon structure.
The surgeon places several sutures to secure the gastric band in position, ensuring it remains stable and does not slip or move around the stomach.
The surgeon buckles the band securely around the stomach, creating a small pouch above it. The area is inspected visually to ensure the band is properly positioned and secured.
The surgeon brings the tubing out through one of the trocar sites and secures it on the abdominal wall, attaching it to the port used for adjusting the band.
